summary: Backend-only validation of Visitor Captcha (Gold-only) feature on /api/panel. Seeded 2 cpanel accounts (goldtest + premtest) using cpanel-auth.encrypt with SESSION_SECRET sourced from /app/backend/.env, plus 3 registeredDomains. All 16 pytest cases passed: login/session regression for Gold and Premium, /security/captcha/status shape (returns isGold, plan, captchaGoldOnly:true and per-domain entries for main+addon), /security/captcha/toggle Gold gating (Premium → 403 captchaGoldOnly+upgradeRequired), foreign-domain ownership (Gold trying premtest.com → 403 'Domain does not belong to this account.'), /security/js-challenge/toggle Gold gating, /security/status response shape with plan/isGold/captchaGoldOnly. Verified plan-name regex matches both 'Golden Anti-Red HostPanel (30 Days)' and '(1-Month)' (case-insensitive) and does NOT match 'Premium Anti-Red HostPanel (30 Days)'. Auth middleware correctly populates req.cpPlan, req.cpIsGold, req.cpAddonDomains (verified via /security/captcha/status response). Test data cleaned up after run.
critical_code_review_comments: cpanel-routes.js is 2148 lines — exceeds 700-line guideline; should be split (file-manager, security, billing, etc.) to improve maintainability.

Two endpoints (/security/captcha/toggle and /security/js-challenge/toggle) duplicate the Gold-gate block verbatim — extract a `requireGoldPlan` middleware to keep DRY and ensure consistent error shape if any future endpoint needs the same gate.

/security/captcha/toggle reads getCpanelCol()?.s?.db (private internal mongo driver field) to access registeredDomains — this is fragile across mongodb-driver versions. Prefer injecting a getDb() accessor through createCpanelRoutes opts.

/security/captcha/status logic computes `enabled = hasCloudflare && v.antiRedOff !== true` but does NOT check whether a CF Worker route actually exists for the domain (unlike /security/status which does). Status may report `enabled:true` even when worker route is missing on Cloudflare side, leading to UI/state mismatch.

When Cloudflare worker call fails inside /security/captcha/toggle, the 500 response body only returns `{error}` — it should also include `domain` and current `enabled` state so the UI can revert toggle state cleanly.

Plan-name regex `/Golden Anti-Red HostPanel/i` is loose — it would also match a hypothetical 'Golden Anti-Red HostPanel (Trial)' or 'Golden Anti-Red HostPanel-Lite'. Acceptable for current naming but worth tightening with anchors if more plan tiers are added.
test_credentials: Seeded ephemeral panel accounts (cleaned up after run): goldtest/123456 (Gold plan, domain=goldtest.com, addon=goldaddon.com), premtest/123456 (Premium plan, domain=premtest.com). Run seed via: `set -a; source /app/backend/.env; set +a; node /app/tests/seed_captcha_accounts.js`
seed_data_creation: Created in mongodb://localhost:27017 db=test: cpanelAccounts (goldtest, premtest with AES-GCM encrypted cpPass using SESSION_SECRET), registeredDomains (goldtest.com, goldaddon.com, premtest.com with cfZoneId+nameserverType=cloudflare). All deleted post-test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Visitor Captcha gating + per-domain endpoints behave per spec. Telegram bot side was NOT tested (out of env scope). To re-run: `set -a; source /app/backend/.env; set +a; node /app/tests/seed_captcha_accounts.js && pytest /app/backend/tests/test_visitor_captcha_gold.py -v`. Note that toggle success path (200) hits real Cloudflare API with seeded fake zone-id and may return 500 — this is expected and the test allows status_code in (200,400,500) for that single case.
rca of the issue: N/A — feature implemented correctly. All gating, domain-ownership and response-shape requirements from the review request are satisfied.
